skill.name: amazon-asin-lookup-api-skill
skill.description: Retrieves structured Amazon product data for a supplied ASIN through the BrowserAct Amazon ASIN Lookup workflow.
skill.summary: Look up Amazon title, price, ratings, availability, and catalog metadata from an ASIN.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The critical static heuristic is not confirmed as malicious: the network calls, local script execution, and environment variable access match the declared BrowserAct ASIN lookup workflow. The skill still presents medium risk because it sends ASIN workflow data to a third-party API and includes guidance that may lead users to share an API key in chat.

medium_findings.title: API Key Handling Guidance May Expose Secrets
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to ask for the BrowserAct API key when the environment variable is missing, and the script output says the user may provide it in chat. This is not confirmed exfiltration, but it encourages secret disclosure outside a dedicated secrets store.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The guidance explicitly references the BrowserAct API key and suggests asking the user or providing it in chat. The code does not transmit the key anywhere except BrowserAct, so the risk is credential exposure through handling practice rather than confirmed theft.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Workflow With Authorization Header
medium_findings.description: The Python script sends ASIN workflow requests to BrowserAct and polls for task status and output using a bearer token. This matches the stated function, but users should understand that product lookup inputs and workflow metadata leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The requests.post and requests.get calls are direct evidence of outbound network activity with an Authorization header. The endpoint is the declared BrowserAct API, so this is legitimate but still privacy and credential-sensitive.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Mostly Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution markers in SKILL.md are Markdown inline code and fenced code examples, not Ruby backtick execution. The only real command guidance is a local Python script invocation using an ASIN argument.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation formatting or a simple python command example. No Ruby runtime or shell backtick operator is present in the file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited SKILL.md lines describe ASIN lookup, product descriptions, and output fields. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, or security-sensitive crypto configuration is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no crypto API or algorithm selection at these locations. The scanner appears to have matched unrelated terms in product-data documentation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Status Checks
low_findings.description: The cited code checks API authorization messages and task identifiers, while the documentation discusses avoiding captcha and retrying authorization failures. No host, user, process, network interface, or filesystem reconnaissance was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to API response handling and user-facing troubleshooting text. I did not find evidence of local system enumeration.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed As Malicious
low_findings.description: The skill combines script execution, network access, and environment variable access, but those behaviors are transparent and required for the BrowserAct lookup workflow. No obfuscation, hidden endpoint, prompt injection attempt, or unauthorized data exfiltration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky capabilities are real, but their purpose is declared in the skill and implementation. Confidence is high enough to dismiss malicious intent, while retaining a medium risk rating for the sensitive behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Used In Outbound Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The script places the BrowserAct API key in an Authorization header for requests to the BrowserAct API. This is expected for the workflow but increases the impact of endpoint misuse or accidental logging.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Authorization header is built from the supplied API key and used in all API calls. This is a clear sensitive credential handling path.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Collection Suggested Through Chat
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup instructions and error output may direct users to provide an API key in the conversation instead of a secure environment variable. Marketplace guidance should warn users to configure secrets out of band.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited text directly mentions asking for or providing the API key. It is a handling risk even though the script itself only reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment.

content.user_title: Look Up Amazon Products by ASIN
content.value_statement: Product teams often need current Amazon data, but manual lookup is slow. This skill uses BrowserAct to retrieve structured title, price, rating, availability, and catalog details from an ASIN.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python helper script with a user-supplied ASIN.
Starts a BrowserAct Amazon ASIN Lookup workflow template.
Polls BrowserAct task status until completion or timeout.
Returns product title, URL, brand, pricing, ratings, and selected attributes.
Reports missing or invalid BrowserAct API key errors.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.
Sends lookup requests and task metadata to BrowserAct.
Depends on BrowserAct and Amazon page availability.
Processes one ASIN per script execution.

use_cases.title: Enrich Product Catalogs
use_cases.description: Retrieve Amazon titles, brands, prices, ratings, and product attributes for known ASINs.

use_cases.title: Check Competitive Pricing
use_cases.description: Look up current Amazon prices for selected products before updating internal pricing records.

use_cases.title: Validate Product References
use_cases.description: Confirm that an ASIN resolves to the expected product details before publishing marketplace content.

prompt_templates.title: Look Up One ASIN
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up Amazon product details for ASIN B07TS6R1SF and summarize the title, price, rating, and availability.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Catalog Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ASIN lookup skill for this ASIN and return the product title, brand, current price, rating count, description, and special features.

prompt_templates.title: Review Price And Rating
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this ASIN with BrowserAct and compare the current price, original price, discount, average rating, and rating count.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Catalog Update Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the ASIN lookup, identify fields useful for a catalog update, flag missing values, and provide concise update notes for the product team.

output_examples.output: The lookup returns the product title, Amazon product page URL, brand, current price, rating average, rating count, and selected product attributes.

output_examples.input: Check whether this ASIN has pricing and review data.
output_examples.output: The result includes whether pricing fields are present, the visible current price, the average rating, and the total rating count.

output_examples.input: Summarize product details for catalog enrichment.
output_examples.output: The response can be converted into a concise catalog note with title, brand, description, features, material, style, and compatibility details.

best_practices: Store BROWSERACT_API_KEY as an environment variable, not in chat.
Validate the ASIN before running the lookup script.
Review returned product data before updating customer-facing systems.
anti_patterns: Do not paste BrowserAct API keys into prompts or shared logs.
Do not use the skill for sensitive or private product research without reviewing BrowserAct data handling.
Do not assume every Amazon page exposes all pricing, rating, or attribute fields.

faq.question: What does this skill look up?
faq.answer: It looks up Amazon product details for a supplied ASIN through BrowserAct.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY to be configured in the environment.

faq.question: Does it scrape Amazon directly from my machine?
faq.answer: No. The local script calls the BrowserAct workflow API, which performs the lookup workflow.

faq.question: Can it process many ASINs at once?
faq.answer: The included script accepts one ASIN per run. Batch use requires external orchestration.

faq.question: What security warning should users know?
faq.answer: The skill uses outbound network calls and an API key. Configure the key securely and avoid sharing it in chat.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
